title: Compiler Messages
text: Compiler Messages Understand the error, warning, or information messages that might display for the compiler. Search: Column Message Explanation Filter results... 60 rows found Message Explanation Filter 60 rows found Filter 60 rows found Message Explanation Error: 1000 - Proc syntax error Generic compilation message issued for syntax or semantic errors. Error: 1000 - 'else' without corresponding 'if' Compilation message. You coded an else without a corresponding if . Error: 1000 - 'endif' without corresponding 'if' You coded an endif without a corresponding if . Error: 1000 - 'endwhile' without corresponding 'while' You coded an endwhile without a corresponding while . Error: 1000 - ‘until’ without corresponding ‘repeat’ Compilation message. You coded an until without a corresponding repeat . Error: 1000 - End of Proc module: ‘endif’ expected. Compilation message. The endif is missing from an if construction. Error: 1000 - End of ProcScript module: 'endwhile' expected. The endwhile statement is missing from a while construction. Error: 1000 - End of ProcScript module: 'until' expected Compilation message. The until statement is missing from a repeat construction. Error: 1000 - Proc error: [line number]: [syntax error] Compilation message that specifies the location of a ProcScript syntax error. Uniface found one of the following syntax errors: Too many labels. Illegal if / else is active. Label not found. Incorrectly coded ProcScript statement. To leave a trigger before correcting the ProcScript code, use a semicolon ( ; ). Warning: 1000 - Field references cannot be optimized; generate Proc listing You referenced one or more fields in ProcScript that the compiler cannot optimize. This means that the compiler cannot uniquely identify the field or fields concerned. Use the format Field.Entity to add the entity name to the field name (this is good programming practice when you explicitly refer to a field). Error: 1000 - Component variable VariableName not allowed A component variable was referenced in a ProcScript module that does not belong to the component where the component variable was defined. Remove the reference to the component variable in that ProcScript. Error: 1000 - Component variable VariableName not found A component variable was referenced in a ProcScript that does not belong to the component where the component variable was defined. Remove the reference to the component variable in that ProcScript. Error: 1000 - Missing period (.) after component instance name Self-explanatory. Error: 1000 - Operation name not valid An operation name must begin with a letter, and contain a maximum of 31 letters, digits, and underscore characters. Error: 1000 - Missing "(" after operation name Self-explanatory. Error: 1000 - Missing comma (,) between parameters Self-explanatory. Error: 1000 Missing ")" after parameters Self-explanatory. Warning: 1000 - Text following ENDPARAMS ignored Text following endparams is regarded as comment, even though it does not start with a semicolon ( ; ). Error: 1000 - Unrecognized parameter type The specified data type for the parameter is not supported. Error: 1000 - Entity name not valid Self-explanatory. Error: 1000 - Data type for field parameter ParameterName does not match data type in component The data type specified for the component variable parameter in a params block does not match the data type declared for the component variable in the component. Error: 1000 - Data type for component variable parameter ParameterName does not match data type in component The data type specified for the component variable parameter in a params block does not match the data type declared for the component variable in the component. For example, the following declaration will result in this error if $NUMERICLOCVAR$ is a Numeric type component variable. For example: STRING $NUMERICLOCVAR$ : IN Copy Error: 1000 - Component variable VariableName not found The component variable declared as a parameter in a params block does not exist. Error: 1000 - Parameter name not valid A parameter name must begin with letter; a maximum of 31 letters, digits or underscore characters is allowed. Error: 1000 - Missing colon (:) after parameter name Compilation message. A colon is required between the parameter name and direction. Error: 1000 - Parameter direction not recognized Parameter direction must be IN , OUT or INOUT . Warning 1000: Text following parameter direction is ignored The text following a parameter declaration is regarded as comment, even though it does not begin with a semicolon ( ; ). Error: 1000 - Variables $0, or $X where X > 99 are not allowed in Self-Contained ComponentType components You cannot use variables $1 through $99 in self-contained components, that is, when the Self-Contained component property is selected. Error: 1000 - Global variables are not allowed in Self-Contained ComponentType components You cannot use global variables in self-contained components, that is, when the Self-Contained component property is selected. Error: 1000 - $result / $101 may not be used as destination in Self-Contained ComponentType components Compilation message. You cannot use $result in self-contained components, that is, when the Self-Contained component property is selected. Error: 1000 - $selblk / $102 is not allowed in Self-Contained ComponentType components Compilation message. You cannot use $selblk in self-contained components, that is, when the Self-Contained component property is selected. Refer to the "Self-Contained" topic. Error: 1000 - $format / $103 is not allowed in Self-Contained ComponentType components Compilation message. You cannot use $format in self-contained components, that is, when the Self-Contained component property is selected. Error: 1000 - $text is not allowed in Self-Contained ComponentType components Compilation message. You cannot use $text in self-contained components, that is, when the Self-Contained component property is selected. Error: 1000 - $prompt is not allowed in Self-Contained ComponentType components Compilation message. You cannot use $prompt in self-contained components, that is, when the Self-Contained component property is selected. Error: 1000 - Entity name required Self-explanatory. Error: 1000 - Occurrence name required Self-explanatory. Error: 1000 Occurrence name not valid Self-explanatory. Error: 1000 - Redeclaration of variable "x" not allowed You may not declare a variable more than once. Error: 1000 - Duplicate definition of module ModuleName found; previous definition will be overwritten The component contains another ProcScript function or operation with the same name. At runtime, Uniface will use the last module it finds. Error: 1000 - Syntax error (Function not valid) Compilation error indicating that the label referenced by the goto ProcScript statement is too long. Error: 1000 - Syntax error (Label not valid) The ProcScript label is longer than 128 characters Error: 1000 - Syntax error (Statement not valid) The syntax of the ProcScript statement is invalid. This error can occur in many circumstances. For example, if you have a conditional statement such as if , while , for , or until and there is no valid conditional expression. Error: 1000 - Syntax error (Operand not valid) An expression contains an invalid operand or is missing an operand. This error occurs if a line of code ends with an operator that should be followed by an operand. For example: In a condition: if vOne > ; Some code endif Copy In an assignment: FIELD.DUMMY = 1+ Copy Warning 1000: - Entry EntryName from model overwrites definition from component. In future Uniface versions, this will be reversed This compiler message is generated in Uniface 9 when duplicate entries are detected in the model and in the component. Problem: ProcScript inheritance has changed in Uniface 10, from being container-based in Uniface 9, to being module-based in Uniface 10. This has consequences for the inheritance of entries and the order in which entries are compiled into the component. In Uniface 9, entries defined in standard triggers and Local Proc Modules of modeled objects are not inherited if any code is defined in the equivalent local containers. In Uniface 10, the compiler concatenates inherited entries then local entries, per object. If the same entry is found in multiple locations, the last entry encountered is used. Resolution: To resolve the issue, review your code and refactor it—move the entry that needs to be compiled to a location where it will be compiled in Uniface 10. Note: We recommend that you define entries in the Script container of the component object. This makes it easier to centralize, locate, and maintain these modules. Refer to the "Entries" topic. Error: 1000 - Function $callup is not allowed in this module The $callup function is allowed only in the following triggers: detail trigger of Field help trigger of Field menu trigger of Field, Entity, or Component receiveMessage trigger of Component pulldown trigger of Component It is not allowed at the most generic level of these triggers, thus not in: detail trigger of Entity help trigger of Entity menu trigger of Application Shell receiveMessage trigger of Application Shell pulldown trigger of Application Shell Refer to the "$callup" topic. Error: 1000 - Keyword 'undeclare' should be followed by a module declaration keyword When undeclaring a module, the keyword undeclare must be followed by they type of declaration; one of function , entry , trigger , operation , webtrigger , or weboperation . Refer to the "undeclare" topic. Warning: 1000 - Ignoring undeclare; ModuleType ModuleName has not been declared yet The compiler has encountered an undeclare instruction for a ProcScript module that has not yet been declared. It may also occur if you have migrated from Uniface 9 using multiple files, which is not recommended. Where necessary, the migration inserts undeclare statements to ensure compatible default trigger behavior between Uniface 9 and 10. To resolve the problem, remove the undeclare statements that caused them. Warning: 1000 - ModuleType ModuleName has been declared after being undeclared This message is generated when the compiler encounters a ProcScript module declaration after that module has been undeclared using the undeclare keyword. It is assumed that if a module has been undeclared (typically, to make a trigger revert to its default behavior), declaring the module again is probably a mistake. Refer to the "undeclare" topic. Error: 1000 - oneModuleType moduleName has been undeclared, but moduleName was declared as otherModuleType Triggers and web triggers share the same namespace, as do operations and web operations, so it is possible to overlay a trigger declaration with a webtrigger declaration (and vice versa), or a weboperation with an operation (and vice versa). However, when using undeclare , you need to be clear about which declaration is being undeclared. Refer to the "undeclare" topic. Warning: 1000 - ModuleType ModuleName has already been undeclared Self-explanatory. Info: 1000 - Skipping ModuleType ModuleName declared in component; it has been undeclared A ProcScript module that has be undeclared using the undeclare keyword is not compiled. Refer to the "undeclare" topic. Error: 1000 - Undeclare of triggers and webtriggers is not allowed in this container Triggers and web triggers cannot be declared or undeclared in the Declarations container. Error: 1000 - Undeclare of operations is not allowed in this container Operations cannot be declared or undeclared in the Declarations container. Error: 1000 - Undeclare of weboperations is not allowed in this container Web operations cannot be declared or undeclared in the Declarations container. Warning 1000: - Include library name LibraryName will be truncated to TruncatedName The maximum length of Include Library names is 60 bytes. If a name is longer than this, it is automatically truncated. When referenced by an #include instruction, Uniface attempts to retrieve the specified IncludeScript using the truncated names. As long as such an IncludeScript exists, it is included in the code. Refer to the "#include" topic. Warning: 1000 - IncludeScript name IncludeScriptName will be truncated to TruncatedName The maximum length of IncludeScript names is 60 bytes. If a name is longer than this, it is automatically truncated. When referenced by an #include instruction, Uniface attempts to retrieve the specified IncludeScript using the truncated names. As long as such an IncludeScript exists, it is included in the code. Refer to the "#include" topic. Info: 1000 -(Preprocessor) #INCLUDE IncludeProcName Not Found The specified Include Proc cannot be found. This may be because: The applicable Include Proc does not exist. The name of the include Proc is incorrectly specified. The Include Proc is located in a different library. The #include statement specifies an Include Proc or library name that was truncated to 16 bytes in Uniface 9. In Uniface 10, the maximum length of IncludeLibrary names and IncludeScript names is 60 bytes. For example, given an Include Proc called VeryLongLibraryName:VeryLongIncludeProcName , Uniface 9 would truncate it to VeryLongLibraryN.VeryLongIncludeP . The following #include instruction would succeed because it would look for the truncated name: #include VeryLongLibraryName:VeryLongIncludeProcName Copy After migration to Uniface 10, the same instruction fails, because it is looking to include: VeryLongLibraryName:VeryLongIncludeProcName . However, the migrated IncludeScript is still called VeryLongLibraryN:VeryLongIncludeP . Warning: 1000 - Script fragment seen outside a module declaration. This message occurs when ProcScript is encountered outside of a trigger, operation, or function declaration, that is, before a function , trigger , or operation statement, or after an end statement. Error: 1000 - Cannot find include script The specified Include Proc for the UX widget in the physical web widget definition (web.ini) file cannot be found. This may be because: The applicable Include Proc does not exist. The name of the include Proc is incorrectly specified. The Include Proc is located in a different library. Error: 1000 - Public web access is not permitted for system triggers This message occurs when a system trigger is defined with the Public Web qualifier, resulting in a compilation failure for the Uniface component. Related Links Self-Contained Determines whether the component can run on a server without reference to global objects or a user interface. Valid values are T or F (default). Entries Entries are legacy ProcScript modules defined by the entry ProcScript statement. They can be used as private functions, or they can be executed in response to a call statement. $callup For the trigger that is currently being executed, call that same trigger one level up in the hierarchy. If the trigger is not defined one level up, then the next available level up is called (if applicable). undeclare Force the specified ProcScript module to be ignored during compilation. #include Insert the contents of the specified IncludeScript in the location of the #include directive.
